title: alnus crispa var. mollis
intro: Alnus crispa var. mollis, also known as the Sitka alder, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to coastal areas of western North America. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Alnus crispa var. mollis are often attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Alnus crispa var. mollis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and skin conditions.
